title: Apply SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21 P0/P1 deltas to SPEC.md
description: Resolve 4 P0 + 14 P1 + trivial P2/P3 findings from SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md. Decisions from user: witness=local SHA-256, at-rest=FileVault only, bundle=dev.friquelme.shotfuse, SCA=surface prompt never auto-modify, keymap=restart-only, license=MIT. Spec bumps 0.1.0→0.1.1.
acceptance_criteria: SPEC.md spec version 0.1.1; sections §4 (Witness/Echo), §5 (invariants 1/7/8/10/11/12), §6 (UUIDv7, display object, OCR wrapper, annotation defaults), §7 (bundle IDs, side-effect policy), §13 Privacy, §14 Platform features, §15 System integration, §16 CLI, §17 Observability all updated. Audit score target ~82/100.
close_reason: SPEC.md v0.1.1 written. All 4 P0s, 14 P1s, and trivial P2/P3s applied. Bundle ID dev.friquelme.shotfuse, license MIT, witness=local SHA-256, at-rest=FileVault, SCA=surface prompt, keymap=restart-only all locked.

title: Author Shotfuse v0.1 spec (tighten BRAINSTORM.md into executable contract)
description: BRAINSTORM.md is rich but it's a menu, not a contract. Produce a tight v0.1 spec that locks: (1) Definition of Done for Weekend 1 and Weekend 2 as single observable behaviors, (2) explicit non-goals list, (3) resolutions or deferrals for open questions in BRAINSTORM.md, (4) named vocabulary fixed in code (Limbo, Fuse, Witness Capture, Capture Echo, Decay Gradient, .shot package format), (5) v0.1 scope boundaries so scope drift is visible. This spec becomes the reference contract for all phase plans.
acceptance_criteria: Spec file exists at repo root. Has Definition of Done for v0.1. Has explicit non-goals. Locked vocabulary section references .shot package format and Shotfuse brand. Open questions from BRAINSTORM.md are all either answered or explicitly deferred with trigger conditions.

title: Adversarial audit of SPEC.md v0.1
description: Run octo:spec-style completeness challenge against SPEC.md. Surface missing requirements, contradictions, edge cases. Produce scored audit + proposed delta in SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md. Non-destructive — do not modify SPEC.md.
acceptance_criteria: SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md exists with dimensional scoring, severity-ranked findings, and concrete delta proposals. SPEC.md unchanged.
close_reason: SPEC-AUDIT-2026-04-21.md written. 53/100 score, 4 P0 blockers, 14 P1/P2/P3, paste-ready deltas for all non-P3 findings.

value: Product name: Shotfuse (decided 2026-04-21 via multi-AI naming brainstorm). App display name Shotfuse, CLI binary shot, package extension .shot. Pattern follows GitHub/gh and Homebrew/brew: brand name for README/marketing, short verb for daily use. GitHub user 'shotfuse' is squatted by inactive account — publish as github.com/<owner>/shotfuse or use an org like shotfuse-app if needed. shotfuse.com domain available. No Homebrew or App Store collision.

Platform target: macOS 26+ (raised floor from brainstorm's original 14+ on 2026-04-21). This unlocks native SCRecordingOutput, captureMicrophone, showMouseClicks, includeChildWindows, SensitiveContentAnalysis — no re-implementation of click rings. Stack: Swift + SwiftUI (settings/inspector) + AppKit (capture overlays, Limbo HUD, annotation canvas where focus/coordinate determinism matters). Test framework: Swift Testing. Single CaptureEngine actor owns state machine; SwiftUI views never own SCStream.

Rejected names (do not re-suggest): Ferry, Relay, Mote, Trail, Ephemera, Witness (too metaphorical — did not read as capture tool at a glance). Shutter (GitHub collision with active Linux screenshot tool shutter-project/shutter, 633 stars, maintained). Lightbox (jQuery Lightbox owns SEO). Frame (too generic to rank). Prism (prism.js owns namespace). Runner-ups seriously considered in final round: Dailies (film-production vocab, 24h fuse thesis match) and Plate (master+derived package format match). Chose Shotfuse for legibility — 'shot' reads instantly as capture, 'fuse' carries expiry. Full brainstorm artifacts: BRAINSTORM.md in repo root plus multi-AI round outputs in /tmp tasks/ dir.

Repo layout: Xcode workspace at repo root with split targets — App/ (menu-bar UI), CLI/ (shot binary), and Core/ as a local Swift Package for capture engine, package format, indexing. Willingness to invest upfront for long-term maintainability confirmed by user. Distribution: open source, public GitHub, free for everyone. No commercial or subscription pressure.
